label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the passage. The question is about what we know about the garage sale, and the options are A to D. First, the passage mentions that garage sales are usually held in the spring and fall when the weather is still good. So option A says "only held in the spring," but the passage says both spring and fall, so A is incorrect. Option B states that the goods are totally useless. The passage actually says, "having decided that he would like to clear his residence of accumulated items which are no longer useful to him." The key here is "no longer useful to him," but that doesn't mean they are totally useless to others. Many items can still be useful to buyers. So B is probably incorrect. Option C says garage sales are popular with Americans. The passage states, "Perhaps even more enjoyable than flea markets are the weekend outdoor garage sales which have now become very much a part of American culture." That clearly indicates popularity, so C seems correct. Option D claims all goods are sold far below true value. The passage
